name: Pico Sacro and Ulla River Loop Trail
name_original: Rivadulla – sendero de Río Ulla por Monte Sacro
description: Hike the difficult 13.9-mile Rivadulla – Ulla River trail through Monte Sacro, featuring 2083 feet of elevation gain and river views.
region_intro: Hiking around Vedra offers a diverse landscape characterized by river valleys, rolling hills, and forested areas. The region features a network of trails that often follow waterways like the Ulla River and lead to scenic viewpoints. Elevation changes are moderate, with some routes including steeper ascents to prominent hills. The terrain primarily consists of natural paths through woodlands and open countryside. Best hiking trails around Vedra The most popular hiking route is San Xoán da Cova Trail, a 11.6 miles (18.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 4 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores varied terrain, including river sections and forested paths. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico Sacro and Ulla River Loop Trail, a difficult 13.9 miles (22.4 km) path. This route combines river views with an ascent to Pico Sacro, offering panoramic vistas. Local hikers also love the Gardens of Pazo de Ortigueira – Gundián Viewpoint loop from Campeliños, an 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail leading through historical gardens and to a prominent viewpoint, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around Vedra is defined by river valleys, forested hills, and scenic viewpoints, with options ranging from easy walks to more challenging routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely strolls to demanding full-day hikes. The routes in Vedra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 800 reviews. More than 5,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Vedra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Vedra?
A: Vedra offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 220 routes to explore. These trails traverse diverse landscapes, including river valleys, rolling hills, and forested areas.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on hikes around Vedra?
A: Hiking around Vedra features varied terrain, primarily consisting of natural paths through woodlands and open countryside. Many routes follow waterways like the Ulla River, and some include steeper ascents to prominent hills, offering scenic viewpoints. Elevation changes are generally moderate.

Q: Are there easy hiking options in Vedra suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Vedra provides numerous easy hiking options. Out of the over 220 routes, 100 are classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners, families, or those looking for a leisurely stroll. These trails often feature gentle gradients and well-maintained paths.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes in Vedra?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Vedra offers several difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991774007/san-xoan-da-cova-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Xoán da Cova Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an 11.6-mile (18.7 km) route exploring varied terrain, including river sections and forested paths. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991760045/pico-sacro-and-ulla-river-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Sacro and Ulla River Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.9-mile (22.4 km) path combining river views with an ascent to Pico Sacro for panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Vedra?
A: Yes, many of Vedra's trails are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40638405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gardens of Pazo de Ortigueira – Gundián Viewpoint loop from Campeliños&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.8-mile (14.2 km) trail leading through historical gardens and to a prominent viewpoint.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints along the trails?
A: Hikers can enjoy several natural attractions and viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2784451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Sacro&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views, especially when combined with the Ulla River trails. You can also find beautiful forest areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5293363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carballeira de San Lourenzo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2927035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alameda&lt;/a&gt;, providing serene natural settings for your walks.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I visit near Vedra's hiking trails?
A: Vedra is close to significant historical and cultural sites. You can visit the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/107287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santiago de Compostela Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; and explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4528821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Centre of Santiago de Compostela&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4801214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Holy Door (Puerta Santa)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4431417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Raxoi Palace&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or wild swimming spots near Vedra hikes?
A: While the region is characterized by river valleys, specific wild swimming spots or prominent waterfalls are not extensively highlighted in the available route data. However, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14984854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza do Río Pereiro loop&lt;/a&gt; suggests a waterfall (Fervenza means waterfall) along the Pereiro River, offering a scenic natural feature to explore.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Vedra?
A: The hiking trails in Vedra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 800 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, including river sections and forested paths, and the panoramic vistas from elevated points like Pico Sacro. The variety of routes, from leisurely strolls to demanding full-day hikes, is also frequently appreciated.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Vedra?
A: Given the region's characteristics of river valleys and forested areas, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions in Vedra. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant with spring blooms or autumn colors. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring cooler, wetter conditions, though many trails remain accessible.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting on Vedra's trails?
A: Hiking through Vedra's woodlands and along river valleys provides opportunities for wildlife spotting. The diverse ecosystems support various local fauna. Keep an eye out for birds, small mammals, and other native species that inhabit the forests and riverbanks.
tips: According to many, this country house is home to one of the most fascinating gardens in Galicia due to its extraordinary botanical value.
Spectacular viewpoint of the Ulla bridges including the famous arched train bridge
Magnificent waterfall with a meadow for a picnic

An isolated mountain peak, giving it a unique point of reference in the surrounding landscape. The top of Pico Sacro is the first place from where you can see the towers of the church of Santiago de Compostela

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this hiking trail?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult hike, requiring very good physical condition. It's not suitable for beginners or young children due to its significant length and elevation gain. Hikers should be prepared for a challenging experience.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Rivadulla – Ulla River trail?
faqs.answer: Hikers generally complete this trail in approximately 6 hours and 35 minutes. This estimate is for a steady pace and does not include extended breaks.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail features a varied terrain. You'll walk on approximately 11.1 km of road, 9.42 km of dedicated paths (senda), and 1.11 km of narrower trails (sendero), along with some street sections. Expect a mix of surfaces, from paved to natural paths, often through lush forests and along riparian landscapes near the Ulla River.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: As you hike, you'll discover several notable spots. Highlights include the beautiful Gardens of Pazo de Ortigueira, the panoramic Viewpoint of Alto do Castro, the scenic Fervenza de Codeso waterfall, and the prominent Pico Sacro summit.

faqs.question: Is the Rivadulla – Ulla River trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific restrictions are not detailed, many trails in Galicia are dog-friendly. However, given the trail's length and difficulty, ensure your dog is accustomed to long hikes and varied terrain. Always keep your dog on a leash, especially near wildlife or other hikers, and carry waste bags.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Galicia is known for its lush landscapes, which means it can be wet. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring colder, wetter conditions, potentially making some sections muddy or slippery.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Rivadulla – Ulla River trail through Monte Sacro. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking areas for this exact trailhead are not detailed in the available information. However, for trails of this nature in Galicia, it's common to find parking near villages or significant landmarks close to the starting point. We recommend checking local maps or community resources for the most convenient parking options near Rivadulla or the Ulla River access points.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of other official trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Camiño Xabrés (Oeste), the Mtb Camiño Xabrés (Oeste), and smaller sections of the Sendeiro de San Xoán da Cova, A Ponte de Gundián, and Rúa da Raíña Lupa.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear with good ankle support, and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A first-aid kit, navigation tools (like a fully charged phone with the komoot app), and sun protection are also highly recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is a natural route, the region around the Ulla River and Monte Sacro has villages and towns where you can find facilities. It's advisable to check for cafes, restaurants, or accommodation in nearby communities like Rivadulla or other settlements along the Ulla River before or after your hike.
